observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommended the CT and the LO to (a) prepare the consolidated RCD with all the
details and information prescribed and with the formers acknowledgment to establish
accountability and provide check and balance; and (b) COs prepare RCD and remit
the collection daily to the LO.

Recommendation:

We recommend the City Treasurer to submit the CRAAF together with the RAAF to
the Audit Team not later than the fifth (5") day of the ensuing month.

Recommendation:
We recommend the (a) CAO to submit Subsidiary Ledger for CIP and Due to NGA
account; and (b) the CAO and City Engineer to conduct reconciliation of their
reports.

Recommendation:

We recommend that management adhere with the pertinent provisions of COA
Circulars No. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of LDRRMF.

Recommendation:

We recommend the CAO (a) to prepare and submit subsidiary ledger for the STF-
DRRM in accordance with the pertinent provisions of NGAS for LGU, Volume I
Section 107; and (b) to conduct reconciliation between the STF-DRRM account, Cash
in Bank for DRRM and DRRM utilization report.

Recommendation:

We recommend the City Accountant and City Treasurer to (a) continue to be
compliant with the terms and conditions of the loan agreement entered into with the
banks, (b) maintain special accounts in the GF and maintain subsidiary records for
every project funded by the secured loans to easily monitor the status and
implementation of the funded projects; and (c) the City Accountant to submit financial
statement as mention in Section 110 of NGAS for LGU, Volume 1.

Recommendation:

We recommend Management (a) the submission of reviewed GAD annual
Accomplishment Report; (b) to consider the establishment of a GAD office to maintain a
focused, concentrated, and coordinated response in the administration, planning,
implementation, and reporting of GAD related PPAs; and (c) to conduct GAD
assessment tool in the preparation of GAAD budget.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental  budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

We recommended the Treasurer to require the said SDOs to maintain and update their respective cashbook on a daily basis, to close the cashbooks at the end of each month by double-ruling  below the last entries in the cashbook and to closely monitor and ensure the accuracy and physical existence of cash on hand by reconciling the cash on hand with the cash balance in the cashbook. We recommend further to conduct reconciliation of records on a regular basis  between the Treasurers and Accounting Office.

We recommended the Management the following:

a. Require the Accountable Officer to secure the correct cash book form and established the Cashbook as  Cash Disbursing Officer to commence from last examination conducted, and

b. Keep updated cashbook which shall be made available to management and COA in the conduct of audit of cash and accounts of the Accountable officer.

We have reiterated our previous years audit recommendation to the Management as follows:

count  of
property, plant, and
equipment by type
annually and reported
on the Report on the

3. To comply with

006 dated January 31,

(PPE), Recognition of
PPE Items Found at

Disposition for Non-
existing/Missing PPE
Items, for the One-

Account Balances of
Government Agencies.

We recommended the LCE require the City Assessor to prioritize the registration and transfer of title of the land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the City of Lamitan in compliance with
observations.reason: Section 39 of PD No. 1445 and  Supreme Court Decision GR No. 163551.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the agency to strengthen its internal  control  to safeguard its assets and to check the accuracy and reliability of its accounting data. We further recommend the Accounting Office and the CGSO the following:

The Accounting Office to book up supplies, materials, and equipment and relief goods procured out of the agency’s fund under the appropriate Inventory and PPE account. Except for the PPE account, issuance for distribution to end users/beneficiaries shall be supported with Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and taken up under the appropriate expense account.

We have recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to submit the  above-mentioned reports  within  the prescribed  timetables so that timely post-audit of transaction could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

We recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to maintain and submit General and Subsidiary Ledgers for the General and Trust Fund accounts and to strictly comply with the provision of Section 12 of  the MNGAS Volume II, Section 114 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 2010-001.
We recommended that Management  should submit Annual SEF Budgets to the Office of the Auditor on time.

We recommended the Accountant should make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts.  Thereafter, make the necessary adjustment/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balance of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in the recording and classifying the City’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are all the same.

We recommended the Management to release the P40,000.00 retention money to the concerned suppliers in accordance with Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. For the P10,000.00 beginning balance with no existing records, no actual claim was made, and remain outstanding for 14 years or more revert it to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund per Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

For CGSO  to conduct physical count on Land and prepare property card (PC), and Coordination between the CGSO and the Accounting Office and ensure that their records on the lands owned by the City tally.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee was not able to conduct the physical count on Land due to Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: Coordination between the City Accounting Office (CAO), City Assessors Office (CAsO) and the City Government Service Office (CGSO) and ensure that their records on the lands owned by the City tally; Implement a system to link the records of the CAO, CAsO, and the CGSO to make their work more efficient and accurate; and For the CAsO to ensure completeness of information on the Schedule of Lands owned by the City.
observations.reason: Still  verifying and  updating real property and ledger cards and submit it the soonest possible time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the City Government to fast track the implementation program for the generator sets.
observations.managementAction:
City Government of Lamitan successfully conducted  the three (3) days (Feb. 18 — 20, 2021) actual commercial operation - synchronization and  operation on an island mode set up in compliance  to ERC Certificate of Compliance requirements relative to the joint petition of LGU Lamitan and BASELCO for the approval of the Power Supply Agreement  at ERC.

LGU Lamitan next step is the submission  of additional requirements to the ERC-LMMD relative to COC and filing of the joint petition to ERC for the approval of the ePSA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management faithfully adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of LBC 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.
The City Planning and Developmental Coordinator, City Engineer and City Budget Officer be required to fasten the implementation of these projects.

The Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to review and update the LDRRMF to include the activities to be funded by the DRRM STF and to indicate in the said investment plan the specific CO funded by the DRRM Fund and the corresponding amount.
At the end of each year, the City Accountant shall prepare the journal entry to record the transfer of the unexpended portion of the LDRRMF from GF to TF.

Reconcile the balances of the Trust Liability – DRRM, the Report on unutilized DRRM Fund with the Cash-in Bank 5% Calamity Fund to establish a sufficient cash back-up for the said trust account; Trace back the balances of the unutilized DRRM-QRF and MOOE that should be transferred to the Special Trust Fund or should be remain in the GF as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and Require the LDRRMO and the City Accountant to carefully review the balances of the ledgers and schedules and coordinate with the City Treasurer before certifying any reports.
The CGSO to conduct physical count on lands and prepare property card (PC), and Coordination between the CGSO and the CAO and ensure that their records on the lands owned by the City tally.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was not able to conduct the physical count on Land due to Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: Coordination between the CAO, CAsO and the CGSO and ensure that their records on the lands owned by the City tally; Implement a system to link the records of the CAO, CAsO, and the CGSO to make their work more efficient and accurate; and For the CAsO to ensure completeness of information on the Schedule of Lands owned by the City.
observations.reason: Still verifying and updating real property ledger cards and ledger and making reconciliation and submit it the soonest possible time.

observations.recommendation: To maximize the utilization of the GAD through the implementation of GAD related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which fund were provided. We also urged the City Government to abide by the annual GPB for the agency to achieve its objective of strengthening public information on GAD issues and policies.
To submit the above cancelled checks to the Auditor, and To maintain a system of accounting to lessen cancelled checks.

A Special Account in the General Fund be maintained and to submit at the end of the year, post-closing trial balance for each special account together with the following financial statements: Statement of Financial Position, Statement of Financial Performance, and Statement of Cash Flows.
observations.reason: Still coordinating with the Office of the City Treasurer as to what would be included under the special account.

observations.recommendation: All cash receive from the operation of the Hostel be credited to Income from Hostel account and free accommodation of guest/visitors of the City Government should be recorded as part of the income from the Hostel and be charged against Representation expense.
The City Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries as follows:

The Bids and Award Committee (BAC) be required to prepare each semester a PMR, and furnish the COA Audit Team with a copy thereof, which shall serve as an effective tool in the systematic and logical assessment/evaluation of the efficiency and effectiveness of the Agencies Procurement System.

To cause the immediate submission of a complete list of all completed, on-going and those that are to be implemented projects/programs/activities for the year 2020 and prior years containing all necessary information such as: Project Name, Unit/Office/Division, Brief description of the projects/programs/activities, Contractor or Supplier, Mode of procurement, Funding source, Cost of approved budget, Project duration including start and completion dates, and Location. To impose strict implementation of the provision of COA Circular No. 2013 – 004; and Always provide the Audit Team with update information as to the status of the publicity of PPA in order to allow timely validation of the same.
observations.reason: Still in the process of updating the list of completed, on-going and to be implemented projects and to provide necessary details.

observations.recommendation: To observe their duties as defined under COA Circular Nos. 2015-008 and 2016-004. To coordinate with and/or assist the City Accountant in the recognition of infrastructure assets in the books of accounts.

The City Government to fast track the implementation target for the recognition of the infrastructure’s assets in the books of accounts; the inventory of all Infrastructure Assets as defined under COA Circular 2015-008 and 2016-004; and to observe their duties as defined under COA Circular Nos. 2015-008 and 2016-004. To coordinate with and/or assist the City Accountant in the recognition of infrastructure assets in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: City Government successfully conducted the three (3) days (Feb. 18 – 20, 2021) actual commercial operation – synchronization in compliance to ERC Certificate of Compliance requirements relative to the joint petition of the LGU and BASELCO for the approval of the Power Supply Agreement at ERC. The LGU next step is the submission of additional requirements to the ERC-LMMD relative to COC and filing of the joint petition to ERC for the approval of the ePSA.

observations.recommendation: The City Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain the supplies and property ledger cards and property records respectively, to reconcile the amount of actual properties counted with the recorded amount and make the necessary adjustment.
observations.reason: Still in the process of updating the supplies and property ledger cards and records.

We recommended the  City  Accountant  and  General  Services  Officer  to
   maintain the supplies and property ledger  cards  and  property  records,
   respectively, to reconcile the amount of actual properties  counted  with
   the recorded amount or properties in the books of the LGU  and  make  the
   necessary adjustment.

We recommended the City Accountant, to reconcile the  reciprocal  account
   in the general fund maintained by the City, by identifying the causes  of
   discrepancies in the  corresponding  subsidiary  Ledgers  and  to  effect
   immediate proper and verifiable adjustments in  the  books  of  accounts.
   Likewise, periodic reconciliation of the  accounts  should  be  made,  to
   ensure ate accuracy of the reciprocal accounts.

We recommended that at the end of each year the City Accountant  and  the
   CDRRM officer shall transfer all the  unexpended/unobligated  balance  of
   the QRF and the DRRMF - MOOE to the special trust fund under the  account
   “Trust Liability - DRRM”.

We recommended the City Accountant and the CDRRM officer shall faithfully
   adhered all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund as follows:




   a. Submit the  annual  local  disaster  risk  reduction  management  fund
      investment plan (LDRRMFIP)  for 2018 DRRM transaction per COA Circular
      2012 - 002 to support the legality of the 2018 DRRM transaction; and



   b. Submit the LDRRMFIP  for  2017  to  avoid  suspending  the  DRRM  2017
      transaction.

We recommended the  City  Accountant,  CDRRM  Officer  and  the  Property
   Officer  shall  faithfully  adhere  to   all  the   laws  and  regulation
   concerning DRRM fund as follows:




   a. The property officer shall prepare, maintain and submit a report of
      (1) inventory list of items for DRRM, (2) inventory list  of  procured
      items for DRRM and (3) inventory list of donated items  for  DRRM  in
      three (3) copies.  Copies  of  the  report  shall  be  distributed  as
      follows:



           Original Copy - COA Auditor
           Duplicate - Accounting Unit
           Triplicate - Property/ Supply officer



   b. Periodic reconciliation of accounting and property  records  shall  be
      made and discrepancies, if any,  shall  be  immediately  verified  and
      adjusted.

Management must comply with all the requirements of RA 9184 and its implementing rules and regulation, also, all procurement should be diligently, meticulously and judiciously planned by the procuring entity to avoid transaction that are disadvantageous to the entity, and 
Fast track the construction and preparation of the location site for the generator sets.

At the beginning of each year, the City Engineer submit the list of all on 









going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 	6 004.

Submit list of all non 	6 infrastructure asset that are to be implemented during the year and also, submit a quarterly report of PPAs for non 	6 infrastructure PPAs as required in COA Circular 2013 	6 004, section 2.1 and 2.2.4

The City Accountant:
should send follow-up and or demand letters requiring the Lamitan City NHS to liquidate. Should not allow additional cash advance to Lamitan City NHS unless the previous cash advances given to them are first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.

Require the employees and Official concerned to immediately liquidate their cash advances. They should send follow-up and or demand letters requiring the personnel concerned to liquidate. Otherwise, the unliquidated cash advances should be deducted from their salaries and/or other benefits due them.
The accountant should not allow additional cash advance to any official or employee unless the previous cash advances given to them are first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
Maintain separate depository account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

At the end of each year the City Accountant and the CDRRM officer transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF - MOOE to the special trust fund under the account 	3Trust Liability - DRRM	4 and deposit the corresponding amount to the depository account exclusively for the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management fund.
The City Accountant submit a complete and extensive subsidiary ledger for accounts receivable and long term liabilities as required

Direct the Accountant to reflect the current portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt in the Financial Statements (FS), so that its users will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.

Require the City Accountant to derecognize PPE with no future economic benefits or no service potential that is expected from its used.
Instruct the CGSO to exclude in the Report of Physical Count of Property,	Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) any obsolete and unserviceable properties; and
Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable property (IIRUP), copy furnished the Accounting office for derecognition of accounts, and the COA auditor for inspection and eventual appraisal and disposal.

Adhered with the rules and regulation stated in EO 298 and COA Circular 2012 	6 001 to avoid future disallowances and suspension. Meantime settle the current audit disallowance and submit the necessary documents to settle audit suspension.

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that the concerned City Officials remit the unused/unexpended balances of PDAF to the national Treasury as required under Executive Order No. 431 dated May 30, 2005 considering also the Supreme Court decision on porked barrel dated November 19, 2013.
observations.reason: The unutilized balance is still intact and deposited at Land Bank and it will be returned the soonest time possible.

observations.recommendation:
RECOMMENDATION:

Management must comply  with  all  the  requirements  of  RA  9184  and  its
implementing  rules  and  regulation,  also,  all  procurement   should   be
diligently, meticulously and judiciously planned by the procuring entity  to
avoid transaction that are disadvantageous to the entity, and

Fast track the construction and preparation of the  location  site  for  the
generator sets.

RECOMMENDATION:

We recommend that at the beginning of each year, the  City  Engineer  submit
the list of all  on  –  going  government  projects/programs/activities  and
those that are to be implemented during the year of the  agency,  as  stated
in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

RECOMMENDATION:

We  recommend  that  the  management  submit  its  list   of   all   non   –
infrastructure asset that are to be implemented during the  year  and  also,
submit  a  quarterly  report  of  projects/program/activities  for   non   –
infrastructure PPA’s as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004, section  2.1  and
2.2.4

We recommend that the amount of P954,241.21 be charged against the General Fund and the same amount be reverted to the LDRRMF. Henceforth, limit the use of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction management Fund to the purpose allowed by law and regulations.

We recommend that Management direct the City Accountant be required to transfer the unexpended balance of Mitigation Fund – MOOE to the special trust Fund, and the City Accountant together with the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer  reconciled their records and to account for the discrepancy amounting to P773,903.58.

n the preparation of its Annual Budget for CDRRMC, we recommend that Management to submit to the SP the proposal to appropriate Calamity funds solely for Disaster Risk Reduction Activities, such as but not limited to pre-disaster preparedness activities, post-disaster activities and other related   activities   including payment of premiums and calamity insurance. Office Related Expenses should be appropriated under Administrative expenses of the General Fund.

We recommend that the management fill-up the 3 unfilled positions under the LDRRMO for full compliance of the RA 10121, Section 12.b. furthermore be guided also with Sub-Section 2.1 of the same Rule which mandates that hiring of personnel shall conform to CSC rules and policies.
observations.reason: The management had already filled two positions in CDRRMO office while the other two are still in the selection process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of the inventories and movable properties of the city and update the Property Ledger cards to serve as basis of the accountant in coming up with the accurate book value.

We recommend that the City Government of Lamitan
•Instruct the CGSO to exclude in the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) any obsolete and unserviceable properties; and
•Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable property (IIRUP), copy furnished the city accounting office for derecognition of accounts, and the COA auditor for inspection and eventual appraisal and disposal.
•Require the City Accountant to derecognize PPE with no future economic benefits or no service potential that is expected from its used.
observations.reason: The Management had not derecognized PPE in the books of accounts due to pending approval of the Annual report of Physical count of PPE which was submitted to COA dated August 23, 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Accountant and the Heads of Departments enjoin 	all officials and employees to observe prompt liquidation of cash advances 	pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and to 	enforce the appropriate administrative and/or legal sanctions provided under 	said COA circular

We recommend that the City Accountant, to reconcile the reciprocal account in 	all funds maintained by the City, by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary Ledgers and to effect immediate, proper and verifiable adjustments in the books of accounts. Likewise, periodic reconciliation of the accounts should be made, to ensure ate accuracy of the reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: Management partially complied as stated in reply to AOM 2016 -005

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Accountant submit the Subsidiary Schedule of General ledger Accounts for General Fund, Trust Fund and SEF as required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001; and to revise and prepare a 	more detailed Notes to Financial Statements to disclose therein adequate and necessary details pertinent to each account to include, but not limited to, details 	of amounts with abnormal balances, to be able to provide valuable and relevant information to the users of these financial statements.
observations.reason: Management is exerting extra effort to submit subsidiary schedule of General Ledger accounts for GF, TF and SEF. At present, we are on final stage of verifying and consolidating the necessary data to include the 2016 transaction in order to come up with a more detailed Note to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly observed.
